title: COM Definition Schema
description: Schema for ERA game COM (Command) definitions in YAML format. COMs define actions that can be performed in the game, including their costs, conditions, and effects.
x-modding-note: Adding new COMs with existing effect types requires no C# compilation. Adding new effect types requires C# handler implementation (Tier 3).

id.description: Unique COM identifier (ComId). Must match [ComId(N)] attribute in C# (if migrated from C#).

name.description: Display name of the COM (Japanese or localized text)

category.description: COM category for organization and UI grouping

description.description: Optional description or notes about the COM

cost.description: Resource costs required to execute this COM

conditions.description: Conditions that must be met for the COM to be executable (CAN_COM checks)

target.description: Target of the condition (e.g., talent name, flag name, palam index)

value.description: Value to compare against (type depends on condition type)

threshold.description: Numeric threshold for comparison (alternative to value)

effects.description: Effects applied when the COM executes successfully

target.description: Target of the effect (e.g., source index name, exp index name)

value.description: Effect value (type depends on effect type: integer, string, or object)

formula.description: Formula expression for calculated effects (used in source_scale, etc.)

pain.description: SOURCE:苦痛 (pain) value for source-type effects

pleasure.description: SOURCE:快楽 (pleasure) value for source-type effects

fear.description: SOURCE:恐怖 (fear) value for source-type effects

submission.description: SOURCE:従属 (submission) value for source-type effects

lust.description: SOURCE:欲情 (lust) value for source-type effects

love.description: SOURCE:恋慕 (love) value for source-type effects

shame.description: SOURCE:羞恥 (shame) value for source-type effects

dependence.description: SOURCE:依存 (dependence) value for source-type effects

items.x-extensibility: Additional effect properties can be added for custom effect types. Effect handlers must be registered in C# to process custom effect types.

kojo_file.description: Reference to the associated kojo (dialogue) YAML file (optional, inferred from COM id if not specified)

metadata.description: Additional metadata for documentation, tooling, or modding purposes

migrated_from_cs.description: Original C# class path if migrated (for reference)

migration_date.description: Date of migration from C# to YAML

version.description: Version identifier for this COM definition

effect_type_extensibility.description: Effect types can be extended by implementing IEffectHandler in C# and registering in the effect handler registry. Custom effect types should use the 'custom' type with additional properties.

condition_type_extensibility.description: Condition types can be extended by implementing condition evaluators in C# and registering in the condition evaluator registry. Custom condition types should use the 'custom' type with additional properties.
condition_type_extensibility.x-requires: C# condition evaluator implementation + evaluator registration

tier-1.description: Fully moddable - no C# compilation required
examples: Adding new COM with existing effect types

tier-2.description: Parameter adjustment - no C# compilation required
